tool.name: COCO Annotation Visualizer
tool.description: Draw COCO bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and labels.
tool.command: mkdir -p data_files && #if $input.input_kind == "image" #for $indata in $input.source ln -s '$indata' 'data_files/${indata.element_identifier}.${indata.ext}' && #end for #else ln -s '$input.source' 'data_files/${input.source.element_identifier}.${input.source.ext}' && #end if python '$__tool_directory__/coco_annotation_visualizer.py' --annotations '$annotations' --outdir outputs --input_kind '$input.input_kind' #if $input.input_kind == "video" --vid_stride '$input.vid_stride' #end if #if $filter_categories --filter_categories '$filter_categories' #end if --show_bbox '$display.show_bbox' --show_mask '$display.show_mask' --show_labels '$display.show_labels' --show_count '$display.show_count' --mask_opacity '$display.mask_opacity' --bbox_thickness '$display.bbox_thickness' --color_mode '$display.color_mode' --font_scale '$display.font_scale' --output_format '$display.output_format' --only_annotated '$only_annotated' --output_mode '$output_mode.mode' #if $output_mode.mode in ['video', 'both'] --video_fps '$output_mode.video_fps' #end if

param.label: Input type
param.help: Choose either one or more images, or a single video file (not both).

param.name: source
param.label: Input images
param.help: Select one or more images or a list collection. Filenames must match those in the COCO JSON.

validator.message: TIFF images must contain exactly 3 channels (RGB).

param.label: Frame stride
param.help:
N ≥ 1 — Index-based matching (SAM3 tool output):
Process one frame every N frames. Each candidate frame is matched by name using the pattern {video_name}_frame_{index:06d}.
Set N to the same stride value used when generating the COCO file.
⚠ The video filename must match the one referenced in the COCO file (i.e. the name used when running SAM3).
N = 0 — Positional matching:
Ignore filenames entirely. The 1st COCO image entry is applied to the 1st video frame, the 2nd entry to the 2nd frame, and so on.

param.label: Filter categories
param.help: Comma-separated list of category names to display. Leave empty to show all categories. Note: apostrophes (') are not supported in category names.

param.label: Show annotation count
param.help: Draws the total number of visible annotations in the top-left corner of each image.

param.label: Mask opacity
param.help: Transparency of segmentation mask overlays (0 = transparent, 1 = opaque).

param.label: Color mode
param.option: Per category (same color for each class)
Per instance (different color for each annotation)

param.name: mode
param.label: Output mode
param.help: Choose whether to output annotated frames, a compiled video, or both.
param.option: Video (compile all frames into a single video)

param.label: Video frame rate (FPS)
param.help:
Controls the speed of the output video. Set to 0 to auto-detect from the input video (recommended when input is a video file). For image inputs, set to the original acquisition rate (e.g. 25 fps for PAL, 30 fps for NTSC). If left at 0 with image input, 25 FPS is used.
Example: 60 frames at 60 FPS gives a 1 second video, at 10 FPS the same frames give a 6 second video.

param.label: Video frame rate (FPS)
param.help:
Controls the speed of the output video. Set to 0 to auto-detect from the input video (recommended when input is a video file). For image inputs, set to the original acquisition rate (e.g. 25 fps for PAL, 30 fps for NTSC). If left at 0 with image input, 25 FPS is used.
Example: 60 frames at 60 FPS gives a 1 second video, at 10 FPS the same frames give a 6 second video.

param.label: Annotated frames only
param.help:
When checked (default) : only frames that have at least one matching annotation are saved or compiled into the video.
When unchecked: all frames are included

tool.help:
================================= COCO Annotation Visualizer =================================
Overlay COCO annotations (bounding boxes, segmentation masks, category labels) onto your images or videos.
------------------- Inputs -------------------
- **Images**: one or more .jpg / .png / .tiff files, or a Galaxy list collection. - **Video**: a single .mp4 / .avi / .mov / .gif file. - **COCO annotation file**: a JSON file strictly following the COCO format. It must contain three top-level keys:
- ``images``: list of image entries, each with at least ``id``, ``file_name``, ``width``, and ``height``. - ``categories``: list of class definitions, each with ``id`` and ``name``. - ``annotations``: list of annotation entries, each with ``id``, ``image_id``, ``category_id``, and at least one of ``bbox`` (``[x, y, width, height]``) or ``segmentation`` (polygon list or RLE).
------------------- Filtering -------------------
Use **Filter categories** to display only a subset of classes, for example::
elephant, bird, fish
Leave the field empty to display all categories present in the annotation file.
------------------- Display options -------------------
- **Bounding boxes**: colored rectangles around each detected object. - **Segmentation masks**: semi-transparent color fills drawn from polygon or RLE data. - **Labels**: category name drawn above the bounding box. - **Annotation count**: total number of visible annotations shown in the top-left corner. - **Color mode**: *per category* assigns a fixed color to each class; *per instance* gives each annotation its own color.
------------------- Output mode -------------------
Choose how the annotated frames are returned:
- **Frames** *(default)*: a Galaxy list collection of annotated images, one per input image or stride frame (PNG or JPEG). - **Video**: a single MP4 video compiled from all frames. For video input, all frames are included (non-stride frames are passed through without annotations). - **Both**: the frame collection *and* the video.
When compiling a video, **FPS = 0** auto-detects the frame rate from the input video. For image inputs, set it to match the original acquisition rate (e.g. 25 fps for PAL).
